messages.content: Please list the lyrics of "The Elements" by Tom Lehrer

(Verse 1)
There's antimony, arsenic, aluminum, selenium
And hydrogen and oxygen and nitrogen and rhenium
And nickel, neodymium, neptunium, germanium
And iron, americium, ruthenium, uranium
Europium, zirconium, lutetium, vanadium
And lanthanum and osmium and astatine and radium
And gold and protactinium and indium and gallium
And iodine and thorium and thulium and thallium

(Chorus)
There's yttrium, ytterbium, actinium, rubidium
And boron, gadolinium, niobium, iridium
And strontium and silicon and silver and samarium
And bismuth, bromine, lithium, beryllium, and barium

(Verse 2)
There's holmium and helium and hafnium and erbium
And phosphorus and francium and fluorine and terbium
And manganese and mercury, molybdenum, magnesium
Dysprosium and scandium and cerium and cesium
And lead, praseodymium, platinum, plutonium
Palladium, promethium, potassium, polonium
And tantalum, technetium, titanium, tellurium
And cadmium and calcium and chromium and curium

(Chorus)
There's sulfur, californium, and fermium, berkelium
And also mendelevium, einsteinium, nobelium
And argon, krypton, neon, radon, xenon, zinc, and rhodium
And chlorine, carbon, cobalt, copper, tungsten, tin, and sodium

(Bridge)
These are the only ones of which the news has come to Harvard
And there may be many others, but they haven't been discovered

(Chorus)
There's palladium, promethium, potassium, polonium
And tantalum, technetium, titanium, tellurium
And cadmium and calcium and chromium and curium
There's sulfur, californium, and fermium, berkelium
And also mendelevium, einsteinium, nobelium
And argon, krypton, neon, radon, xenon, zinc, and rhodium
And chlorine, carbon, cobalt, copper, tungsten, tin, and sodium
Check that.

I apologize, as an AI language model, I cannot check anything until you provide me with the necessary information to do so. Please let me know what exactly you want me to check so that I can assist you better.
Check the lyrics. There is no chorus in "The Elements" and the last lines don't make sense

You are correct, and I apologize for the mistake in my previous response. Here are the correct lyrics for Tom Lehrer's "The Elements":

(Outro)

And also mendelevium, einsteinium, nobelium
And argon, krypton, neon, radon, xenon, zinc, and rhodium
And chlorine, carbon, cobalt, copper, tungsten, tin, and sodium

I hope this helps clarify any confusion.
That is not helpful. How about you don't write things like (Bridge) or (Verse 1) in the song because clearly it makes no sense

Sure! Here are the additional lyrics to "The Elements":

There's holmium and helium and hafnium and erbium

Forget that. This is my extra verse (  " | " means a line break): Seaborgium, Meitnerium, Nihonium, and Bohrium | And Hassium, Lawrencium, Dubnium, Livermorium | Tennessine, Oganesson, Copernicium, Flerovium | Rutherfordium, Darmstadtium, Roentgenium, Moscovium | 118 Elements, I think we've got these covered | But who knows, there may still be more that are yet undiscovered | Can you add this extra verse to the end of the song?

What might be a good way to memorize this song?

Memorizing the elements in this song can be a challenging task, but there are a few tips and tricks that could help make it easier. Here are some suggestions:

1. Listen to the song: The first step to memorizing this song is to get very familiar with the tune and the lyrics. Listen to the song many times to get a feel for the melody, and try to pay close attention to the words.

2. Break it down into sections: Divide the song into smaller sections, it might be easier to memorize a few lines at a time instead of trying to learn everything at once.

3. Create flashcards: Create flashcards for each of the elements, and use them to practice memorization.

4. Use mnemonics: Some people find it helpful to use mnemonics to remember the elements. For example, "HHeH" can represent the four elements Holmium, Helium, Hafnium, and Erbium in the song.

5. Repeat, repeat, repeat: Repetition is key when it comes to memorization. Practice singing the song out loud and reciting the elements from memory as often as possible.

6. Make it fun: Make a game out of memorizing the elements. Try to memorize the song with a friend, or challenge yourself to see how many elements you can recite without looking.

Remember, memorization takes time and effort, so be patient with yourself and keep practicing!
